EngineBlock:
Core mechanical power source for the frame.
Converts fuel energy into rotational output.
Higher efficiency reduces thermal output.
Battery:
Stores and discharges electrical energy.
Buffers power spikes during high-demand operations.
Capacity degrades with repeated deep discharge cycles.
MechanicalPump:
Circulates hydraulic fluid through the frame's systems.
Required for hydraulic actuator and booster operation.
Flow rate scales with mechanical power input.
AirFilter:
Removes particulates from intake air before combustion.
Degrades in dusty or toxic atmospheric conditions.
Requires periodic maintenance in hostile environments.
AirBreather:
Draws atmospheric oxygen for combustion-based systems.
Non-functional in vacuum or low-oxygen environments.
Improves engine efficiency in standard atmosphere.
Leg Actuator:
Drives articulated limb movement for locomotion.
Torque output determines terrain traversal capability.
Overload causes joint stress and reduced lifespan.
Inventory Container:
Provides internal storage volume for carried items.
Weight affects frame mobility and power consumption.
Shielded variants resist EMP and environmental damage.
Capacitor Bank:
Accumulates charge for rapid high-power discharge events.
Requires recharge time between burst activations.
Over-charging risks arc failure and system damage.
Heat Sink:
Passively dissipates thermal energy into surroundings.
Efficiency drops as ambient temperature rises.
Critical for sustained high-output operations.
Radiator:
Actively expels heat using coolant circulation loops.
Requires pump operation and clear external surface area.
Damage to fins significantly reduces cooling capacity.
Thermal Buffer:
Absorbs short-duration heat spikes to protect subsystems.
Holds thermal energy temporarily before gradual release.
Saturation leads to rapid system-wide temperature rise.
Shield Capacitor:
Powers and sustains the frame's energy shielding layer.
Higher charge levels extend shield duration under fire.
Requires full discharge before recharge cycle can begin.
Point Defense Turret:
Autonomously intercepts incoming projectiles and missiles.
Coverage cone is fixed relative to frame orientation.
High rate of fire drains power and generates heat quickly.
Chaff Dispenser:
Releases metallic particles to confuse tracking systems.
Single-use cartridges require manual reloading.
Ineffective against optically-guided or passive seekers.
Hydraulic Booster:
Amplifies actuator force output using hydraulic pressure.
Requires active pump flow to function.
Enables mobility over steep gradients and heavy loads.
Gyro Stabilizer:
Maintains frame orientation and resists rotational forces.
Critical for accuracy during movement and recoil absorption.
Spin-up time delays effectiveness after activation.
Radar Array:
Detects and tracks nearby objects via radio wave emission.
Range and resolution scale with power input.
Jammable by hostile ECM emitters at close range.
Thermal Sensor:
Detects heat signatures through environmental obstructions.
Effective at locating concealed or camouflaged targets.
Performance degrades in high-ambient-temperature areas.
LIDAR Scanner:
Maps surrounding geometry using laser pulse reflection.
High-precision spatial data for navigation and targeting.
Blocked by heavy particulate matter such as smoke or dust.
Navigation Core:
Processes positional data and plots traversal routes.
Integrates sensor inputs to maintain situational awareness.
Degraded inputs cause routing errors and drift.
Comms Transceiver:
Sends and receives encrypted short and long-range signals.
Required for coordination with allied units and command.
Signal strength subject to atmospheric modifier.
Repair Nanoforge:
Deploys nanoscale repair agents to restore structural integrity.
Self-repair rate scales with available power and efficiency.
Cannot repair damage faster than it is being received.
Anchor Spike:
Drives a hardened spike into terrain to lock the frame in place.
Prevents movement but greatly increases weapon stability.
Retraction takes time and leaves frame immobile briefly.
Subsystem Overdrive:
Temporarily boosts output of linked systems beyond rated limits.
Significantly increases heat and power draw during use.
Extended overdrive risks permanent subsystem degradation.
Reinforced Spine:
Structural core hardening against ballistic and impact stress.
Increases overall frame durability and damage threshold.
Adds mass which reduces agility and power efficiency.
Shock Frame:
Absorbs and disperses kinetic energy from impacts and blasts.
Protects internal components from concussive damage.
Effectiveness decreases as structural integrity drops.
Vernier Boosters:
Small directional thrusters for fine positional adjustment.
Enable airborne maneuvers and rapid lateral repositioning.
Fuel-limited; burn rate scales with thrust demand.
Mini Nuclear Power Plant:
Compact fission reactor providing high sustained power output.
Requires no conventional fuel but generates constant heat.
Shielding damage risks radiation exposure to nearby systems.
Oxygen Generator:
Produces breathable atmosphere from available source materials.
Required for crew survival in vacuum and toxic environments.
Consumes power proportional to output volume.
Recoil Dampener:
Absorbs and counters weapon recoil forces during firing.
Improves accuracy for sustained or heavy-caliber weapons.
Mechanical wear increases with cumulative recoil impulse.
Targeting Module:
Provides fire-control data and weapon system coordination.
Improves projectile lead calculation and lock-on speed.
Signal modifier affects performance against active jammers.
Weapon Cooling Jacket:
Liquid-cools weapon barrels and firing chambers between shots.
Extends sustained fire duration before thermal shutdown.
Requires coolant flow from an active pump system.
Charge Regulator:
Stabilizes and distributes electrical power across systems.
Prevents voltage spikes from damaging sensitive components.
Overload condition triggers automatic shutdown to protect systems.
RailGun Core:
Electromagnetic mass driver for high-velocity projectile launch.
Extreme range and armor penetration at high power cost.
Requires full capacitor charge before each firing cycle.
HeavyRifle Core:
Large-caliber kinetic slug thrower for heavy engagement.
High single-shot damage with significant recoil generation.
Ammo capacity limited by internal storage allocation.
AutomaticRifle Core:
Rapid-fire ballistic weapon for sustained suppressive output.
Moderate damage per round with high rate of fire.
Heat accumulation is the primary operational constraint.
LaserBeam Core:
Continuous-wave laser emitter for precision cutting and targeting.
Damage sustained as long as beam is held on target.
Atmospheric conditions significantly affect beam coherence.
LaserPulse Core:
Fires discrete high-energy laser pulses in rapid succession.
Balances heat output against consistent damage delivery.
Pulse rate adjustable for thermal management.
MissileSingle Core:
Single-launch guided missile system for precision engagement.
Homing capability depends on active targeting uplink.
Low sustained damage but effective against armored targets.
MissileBarrage Core:
Multi-tube salvo launcher for area suppression and saturation.
High burst damage across a wide target zone.
Reload cycle limits sustained engagement capability.
PulseCutter Core:
Short-range directed energy weapon for close combat engagement.
Disrupts electronic systems in addition to physical damage.
Effective against shields; reduced impact on hardened armor.
Power Distributor:
Routes and prioritizes power flow between all frame subsystems.
Enables dynamic reallocation under load or damage conditions.
Failure causes cascade shutdowns across connected systems.
